text: whitehead hemorrhoidectomy, a stepwise demonstration for resident. This is a sixty-eight years old male, had grade four hemorrhoid for decades, and underwent whitehead hemorrhoidectomy under spinal anesthesia and jackknife position.

The surgeon makes an incision at the three o'clock position from the superior part of the dentate line to the skin. Dissection begins at the dentate line and continues around the anal canal using electrocautery and curved scissors. The anal mucosa is carefully dissected from the anal sphincter muscle. The same procedure is performed at the nine o'clock position, resulting in the complete separation of the mucosal flap from the anal sphincter muscle.

The surgeon excises the hemorrhoid tissue from the mucosa flap and marks the neodental line using a marking pen. The redundant mucosal flap is trimmed, revealing the large hemorrhoid tissue. The edge of the mucosal flap is sutured through the sphincter muscle from the midline and re-approximated to the rectal mucosa using interrupted sutures for neodental line reconstruction.
The surgeon completes the procedure by closing the anal skin with Kega sutures, finalizing the neodental line reconstruction. The surgical field shows minimal blood loss.
